name: Suffolk Rusk (English)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 450°F or Mark 8.
2. Sift the flour and salt together into a bowl, then rub in the butter until the mixture resembles fine breadcrumbs.
3. Stir in the beaten egg and sufficient milk or water to make a smooth dough.
4. Roll out on a lightly floured surface to about 1 inch in thickness, then cut into 2 1/2 inch rounds.
5. Place on a greased baking sheet and cook for 10 minutes.
6. Remove from the oven and split each rusk in half.
7. Reduce the oven temperature to 375°F or Mark 5.
8. Return the rusks to the baking sheet, cut side upwards, and cook for a further 10 to 15 minutes until crisp and golden-brown.
9. Cool on a wire rack. Serve with butter and jam at teatime or as a savory with cheese or Bloater Savoury. Suffolk Rusks store well in a tin.
